lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Hotel & Restaurant
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael Gasparelli Lima | Aula 11 -- Hotel & Restaurant

listenings.text: Good evening and welcome to the Hudson Grand Hotel. Thank you for choosing us during your stay in New York. Here is some important information for our guests. Breakfast is served in the main restaurant from six thirty to ten in the morning. There is a business center on the second floor with printers and meeting rooms. If you need any help, please dial zero from your room and the front desk will assist you. Check-out time is twelve noon. We hope you have a pleasant stay. Enjoy your evening.

When I first traveled abroad for work, the hardest part was not the meetings. It was the small things. Ordering dinner alone, asking for a quiet room, or telling the waiter I could not eat something. I used to point at the menu and hope for the best. But slowly, I learned a few simple phrases. I would like a table for one. Could I have the bill, please? Is there any milk? These small words gave me confidence. Now I travel, I order, and I sleep well, because I can finally ask for exactly what I need.

extra_audio.text: First, Rafael arrives at the hotel and tells the receptionist he has a reservation. Then the receptionist confirms his booking and asks for his passport. Next, she gives him the key and explains the breakfast time. After that, Rafael goes to the restaurant and orders dinner from the waiter. Finally, he asks for the bill and thanks the staff for the wonderful service.
